ogTitle: North Kununoppin vs North Lake Grace: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in North Kununoppin and North Lake Grace.
heading: North Kununoppin vs North Lake Grace — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between North Kununoppin, Western Australia and North Lake Grace, Western Australia. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: North Kununoppin has a lower overall climate risk score (33/100) compared to North Lake Grace (41/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is North Kununoppin or North Lake Grace safer from flooding?
faq.answer: North Kununoppin has a flood risk score of 40/100, while North Lake Grace scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: North Kununoppin scores 16/100 for bushfire risk. North Lake Grace scores 51/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in North Kununoppin or North Lake Grace?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. North Kununoppin has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.63% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
